title: PropAnalytics Engine
subtitle: Advanced statistical analysis designed for proprietary trading firm evaluation metrics.
postStreakThresholdTooltipHtml:
The number of consecutive losing trades after which the engine calculates the statistical advantage (Edge) for deploying the strategy.

Example: If you enter 3, the system calculates the average return of the strategy immediately after it has suffered 3 consecutive losses.
startingCapitalTooltipHtml:
The initial account balance of your Proprietary Trading evaluation (e.g., $100,000).

Why: The engine uses this exact dollar amount as the denominator to calculate your maximum percentage drawdown drops accurately.
dailyDdLimitTooltipHtml: The maximum permissible daily balance drop set by the proprietary trading firm.
contractSizeTooltipHtml:
The contract value of the underlying asset used to calculate Margin / True Leverage. This should match your SQX and broker settings.

Example: A standard Forex lot (1.00) has a Contract Size of 100,000. Other symbols like Indices or Crypto might have a contract size of 1.
maxAllowedLeverageTooltipHtml:
The maximum True Leverage limit allowed by your Prop Firm for this symbol.

Example: If the prop firm allows 1:30 leverage for Forex, enter '30'. The system will draw a threshold line on the history chart and detect a BREACH if your open positions ever exceed this margin utilization.
constraintsTooltipHtml: Metrics specifically evaluated by prop firms to determine consistency and safety buffer before failing an account.
metricMaxDailyDrawdownTooltipHtml:
The worst single-day closed-trade loss as a percentage of your starting balance (midnight to midnight).

Example: If you start Tuesday with $100,000, win to $102k, but then close a loser to drop to $96,000, your daily drop is 4%. Note: This metric uses closed trades only - live floating loss may be deeper.
metricDailyLimitHeadroomTooltipHtml:
The safety buffer between your worst historical daily drawdown and the prop firm's daily fail-limit.

Example: If the limit is 5%, and your worst day hit 4.2%, your headroom is only 0.8%. In live trading, slippage can easily eat 0.8%, meaning this strategy is dangerously close to failing.
metricConsistencyRuleComplianceTooltipHt:
Measures the percentage of your total net profit generated by your single best day.

Example: Most prop firms (FTMO, TopStep) require no single day accounts for more than 30-50% of total profit. If you made $10,000 total but $6,000 came from one lucky day, your score is 60% - they will likely reject the account.
dailyProfitLossTooltipHtml: A chronological representation of net daily profit and loss. Green blocks represent profitable days, red blocks represent losing days.
marginLeverageRiskTooltipHtml: Simulates prop-firm margin rules by calculating the True Leverage (Notional Value of all concurrently open trades / Account Balance). Flags if you breach the allowed limit.
portfolioMarginLeverageUnsupported: Margin & Leverage risk simulation is not reliably supported for Portfolios due to dynamic capital allocation across multiple sub-strategies.
postStreakEdgeTooltipHtml:
Determines if there is a statistical edge to waiting for a drawdown before deploying the strategy. Compares baseline returns against returns immediately following X consecutive losses.

Includes: Post-streak Win Rate (% of wins immediately following the streak), Average Losing Streak length, Recovery Time (avg days to new equity high), and Winning Streak Mode.
drawdownDurationTooltipHtml: The longest consecutive period (in days) the strategy remained below its peak equity. Prop firms look for fast recovery times.
holdingTimeBehaviorTooltipHtml: Compares average holding time. Holding losers significantly longer than winners often indicates poor psychological control or flawed stop-loss logic.
consistencyScoreTooltipHtml: Measures the stability of returns. High standard deviation indicates erratic performance. Profitable Days % shows daily consistency.
tailRiskTooltipHtml:
95% VaR: The maximum expected daily loss 95% of the time.
CVaR: The average loss on the worst 5% of days (Tail Risk).
regimePerformanceTooltipHtml: A proxy for market conditions. Identifies what type of market regime (Momentum, Choppy, High Volatility) the strategy thrives or bleeds in.
disclaimerHtml: This analysis is provided strictly for informational and educational purposes within StrategyQuant X. It does not constitute financial, investment, or trading advice. All results, including backtests and simulations, are based on historical data and hypothetical performance, which may not reflect future market conditions. Past performance is not indicative of future results. Trading involves substantial risk, and you should conduct your own independent research and consult a licensed financial advisor before making any investment or trading decisions. This tool utilizes statistical math based on historical sequences; actual market execution may vary significantly.
